Water-Driven Sawmill: Construct a large sawmill powered by river flow. This project can only be built near a river.
A complete sawmill kit with all components for a large river-powered sawmill.
Use: Place near a river to set up the full sawmill structure. Scale: This is a large construction project.
Place the sawmill kit into the environment.
A large, fixed river-powered sawmill built on the riverbank.
Placement: River environments only via blueprint conditions. Operation: Load a log into the sawmill, then press Cut to saw it into planks.
Pack up the sawmill back into a transportable kit.
Saw one log from the inventory into planks using water power.
A channel segment used to carry and direct water flow to river-powered machines.
Use: Build directional between-location races and install local mill race structures.
A river-driven wheel assembly. Built with a mill race as a required subcomponent.
Subcomponent: The mill race is required to build this wheel.
A water-driven mill that grinds millable materials on demand with 4x the capacity of a rotary quern.
Subcomponent: Requires a mill race to construct. Capacity: Can grind 4x the throughput of a manual rotary quern. Operation: Load millable items and press Grind All to process the batch on demand.

A large forge powered by a water wheel driving a blower system that maintains high temperatures using fire. The water wheel drives the blower, which keeps the forge at optimal temperature for metalworking.

Construction: 4-phase build requiring water mill, stones, mud bricks, planks, clay, leather bellows, and plaster. Power: Water-driven blower maintains forge temperature. Purpose: High-temperature metalworking and smelting.
A large precision-cut copper gear for power transmission in the sawmill.
A smaller copper gear for fine power adjustment in the sawmill mechanism.
A precision copper saw blade mounted on the sawmill frame to cut logs.
A cast saw blade that still needs careful whetstone sharpening.

Dig a fishpond near a river in 3 stages using a shovel (3 hours each). Populate it with live pike, perch, and minnow which will grow over time. When the total fish count exceeds 10, the pond becomes stocked and frogs can be caught. The pond freezes in winter.
A pre-stocked fishpond kit ready for placement near a river. Fish populations grow slowly over time.
Use: Place near a river. Fish grow over time. The pond becomes stocked when the total fish count exceeds 10. Fish are caught with a spear.
Place the fishpond near the river.
Break down the kit and recover most of the planks and rope.

A working fishpond with a small population of pike, perch, and minnow. Only those three breed; trout, char, and sturgeon can be stocked separately but do not breed. Once the breeding population exceeds 10 the pond becomes a fully Stocked Fishpond.

Populations: Pike, perch, and minnow each grow at the same slow rate (~2 days per fish per species). Trout / Char / Sturgeon: Each tracked separately (cap 10 each). They do not breed. Catch: Use a spear to catch one fish at a time. Each catch reduces that species' population by 1. Stocked Threshold: When the breeding population (pike+perch+minnow) exceeds 10, the pond transitions to Stocked and frogs can be caught. Water Draw: Drag a water container in to fill it with unsafe water (boil before drinking). Winter: The pond freezes when winter sets in and only ice fishing is available.
Spear a pike from the pond.
Spear a perch from the pond.
Spear a minnow from the pond.
Draw unsafe water from the pond into a container. Boil before drinking.
Abandon the fish and drain the pond back to a fishpond kit.
A mature fishpond rich with pike, perch, and minnow. Trout, char, and sturgeon can also be stocked but do not breed. The thriving ecosystem also attracts frogs to the water's edge.

Populations: Pike, perch, and minnow continue to grow at the same slow rate. Trout / Char / Sturgeon: Each tracked separately (cap 10 each). They do not breed. Catch: Use a spear to catch one fish at a time. Each catch reduces that species' population by 1. Frogs: The Catch Frog action is available while the pond is Stocked (no spear required). Reverts: If the breeding population (pike+perch+minnow) drops below 10 the pond reverts to its working Fishpond state and the frogs depart. Water Draw: Drag a water container in to fill it with unsafe water (boil before drinking). Winter: The pond freezes when winter sets in and only ice fishing is available.
Spear a pike from the pond.
Spear a perch from the pond.
Spear a minnow from the pond.
Catch a frog by hand from the pond's edge.
Draw unsafe water from the pond into a container. Boil before drinking.
Abandon the fish and drain the pond back to a fishpond kit.
The fishpond is frozen over. The fish remain under the ice but no longer grow until spring. Only ice fishing is possible.

Populations: Pike, perch, and minnow are preserved beneath the ice (no growth until thaw). Trout, char, and sturgeon are tracked separately and preserved across the freeze. Ice Fish: Each ice fishing action takes longer than open water and requires a spear. Water Draw: Disabled while frozen. Thaw: The pond returns to its working state automatically when winter ends. Population is preserved across the freeze.
